---
title: "GLAM Parameter Recovery"
author: "Collaborative AI"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{GLAM Parameter Recovery}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5
)

library(fractalGLAM)
library(tidyverse)
library(magrittr)
library(cmdstanr)
library(bayesplot)
library(posterior)

# Ensure CmdStan is available for the vignette build process
tryCatch({
  cmdstanr::cmdstan_path()
}, error = function(e) {
  cmdstanr::set_cmdstan_path()
})

# 1. Simulate Synthetic Data
set.seed(42)

# Simulation Constants
n_subs <- 10          # Number of subjects
n_trials <- 200       # Number of trials per subject
s_const <- 3e-4       # Scaling factor
true_v_val <- 1.5     # Group velocity
true_sigma_val <- 0.2 # Group noise

# 1. Create individual differences in Gamma
# We sample a different 'true gamma' for each person
sub_traits <- tibble(
  subject_id = 1:n_subs,
  true_gamma_sub = runif(n_subs, 0.0, 1.0)
)

sim_data <- expand.grid(subject_id = 1:n_subs, trial = 1:n_trials) %>%
  left_join(sub_traits, by = "subject_id") %>%
  mutate(
    value_left = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
    value_right = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
    gaze_left = runif(n(), 0.1, 0.9),
    gaze_right = 1 - gaze_left,
    v_l_scaled = scales::rescale(value_left, to = c(1, 10)),
    v_r_scaled = scales::rescale(value_right, to = c(1, 10))
  )
sim_data <- sim_data %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Calculate the raw GLAM signal
    signal = (v_r_scaled * (gaze_right + true_gamma_sub * gaze_left)) - 
             (v_l_scaled * (gaze_left + true_gamma_sub * gaze_right)),
    
    # 2. Choice probability 
    # Use signal * 0.5 or higher to ensure choices aren't purely random
    prob_right = plogis(signal * 0.5), 
    choice = rbinom(n(), 1, prob_right),
    
    # 3. Decision Drift (Nu * S * Magnitude)
    # This is exactly how it's calculated in your Stan loop now
    # We add 0.01 floor to match the 'bulletproof' fix in Stan
    drift = true_v_val * s_const * (abs(signal) + 0.01),
    
    # 4. Generate RT using your parameters
    # - The 'location' (mu) of lognormal is log(1/drift)
    # - The 'scale' is true_sigma_val
    rt = rlnorm(n(), meanlog = log(1 / drift), sdlog = true_sigma_val)
  )


# Preview simulated data
head(sim_data)

# 2. Fit Model
# Preprocess
processed_data <- prep_glam_data(sim_data)

# Fit using VB
fit <- fit_glam(
  processed_data, 
  method = "meanfield", 
  iter = 20000, 
  s_val = s_const,
  force_recompile = TRUE,
# Initialize near the group means to avoid the -inf crash
  init = 0.1#, 
)

# mcmc_fit <- fit_glam(
#   processed_data %>% filter(subject_id == 1), 
#   method = "sample",
#   chains = 2,
#   iter_warmup = 1000,
#   iter_sampling = 1000,
#   # These control the sampler efficiency
#   max_treedepth = 12,    # Increased from default 10
#   adapt_delta = 0.95     # Increased from default 0.80
# )

# 3. Verify Parameter Recovery
library(posterior)

# --- 1. Extract Group-Level Means (Behavioral Scale) ---
group_summary <- fit$summary(c("mu_v_raw", "mu_gamma_raw", "mu_sigma_raw")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Transform raw estimates back to behavioral scale
    estimated_val = case_when(
      variable == "mu_v_raw" ~ exp(mean),
      variable == "mu_gamma_raw" ~ plogis(mean),
      variable == "mu_sigma_raw" ~ exp(mean)
    ),
    true_val = case_when(
      variable == "mu_v_raw" ~ true_v_val,
      variable == "mu_gamma_raw" ~ mean(sub_traits$true_gamma_sub),
      variable == "mu_sigma_raw" ~ true_sigma_val
    )
  ) %>%
  select(variable, true_val, estimated_val, q5, q95)

cat("\n--- Group-Level Recovery ---\n")
print(group_summary)

# --- 2. Extract Subject-Level Parameters ---
# Get individual Nu (v) and Gamma
sub_estimates <- fit$draws(c("v", "gamma")) %>%
  as_draws_df() %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with(c("v[", "gamma[")), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "param_raw", values_to = "estimated") %>%
  mutate(
    parameter = gsub("\\[.*", "", param_raw),
    subject_id = as.numeric(gsub(".*\\[|\\].*", "", param_raw))
  )

# Join with Ground Truth Traits
recovery_data <- sub_estimates %>%
  left_join(sub_traits %>% rename(gamma = true_gamma_sub), by = "subject_id") %>%
  mutate(truth = ifelse(parameter == "gamma", gamma, true_v_val))

# --- 3. Visualize Multi-Panel Recovery Plot ---
ggplot(recovery_data, aes(x = truth, y = estimated)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "midnightblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", size = 0.5, se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Subject-Level Parameter Recovery",
    subtitle = "Red dashed line = Perfect Recovery (1:1)",
    x = "Simulated Ground Truth",
    y = "Posterior Mean Estimate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


recovery_metrics <- recovery_data %>%
  group_by(parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    Correlation = cor(truth, estimated),
    MAE = mean(abs(truth - estimated)),
    RMSE = sqrt(mean((truth - estimated)^2))
  )

cat("\n--- Recovery Performance Metrics ---\n")
print(recovery_metrics)

# Extract draws
# draws <- fit$draws(c("gamma", "v")) %>% as_draws_df()
preds <- predict_glam(fit, processed_data, n_sims = 5)

# Plot Subject-Level Gamma Recovery
plot_gamma_performance(fit, processed_data)

##
# Extract Estimated Gammas from the MCMC fit
est_gammas <- fit$summary("gamma") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    subject_id = as.numeric(gsub(".*\\[|\\].*", "", variable))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(subject_id, estimated_gamma = mean)

# Combine with your simulation traits to see who is "High" vs "Low"
recovery_df <- est_gammas %>%
  dplyr::left_join(sub_traits, by = "subject_id")

# 1. Join the estimated gammas into the prediction data
preds_with_gamma <- preds %>%
  dplyr::left_join(recovery_df, by = "subject_id")

# 2. Identify the most biased (low gamma) and least biased (high gamma) subjects
# (Using the recovery_df we created in the previous step)
low_gamma_id  <- recovery_df %>% dplyr::arrange(estimated_gamma) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% dplyr::pull(subject_id)
high_gamma_id <- recovery_df %>% dplyr::arrange(desc(estimated_gamma)) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% dplyr::pull(subject_id)

# 3. Filter and Plot
comp_plot_data <- preds_with_gamma %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% c(low_gamma_id, high_gamma_id)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    delta_val = value_right - value_left,
    # Now estimated_gamma exists here because of the join!
    gamma_label = paste0("Subject ", subject_id, " (Gamma = ", round(estimated_gamma, 2), ")")
  )

ggplot(comp_plot_data, aes(x = delta_val, y = pred_rt)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "midnightblue", size = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~gamma_label, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "How Gamma Affects the RT Hump",
    subtitle = "Higher Gamma = Sharper 'Value Conflict' Peak",
    x = "Value Difference (Right - Left)",
    y = "Predicted Response Time (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Plotting the 'Shifted Humps'
# 1. Join labels to BOTH dataframes
preds_labeled <- preds %>%
  dplyr::left_join(recovery_df, by = "subject_id") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    type = "Predicted",
    delta_val = value_right - value_left,
    gamma_label = paste0("Subject ", subject_id, "\n(Gamma = ", round(estimated_gamma, 2), ")")
  )

obs_labeled <- processed_data %>%
  dplyr::left_join(recovery_df, by = "subject_id") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    type = "Observed",
    delta_val = value_right - value_left,
    gamma_label = paste0("Subject ", subject_id, "\n(Gamma = ", round(estimated_gamma, 2), ")")
  )

# 2. Filter for the extreme subjects we identified earlier
# (Assuming high_gamma_id and low_gamma_id are still in your environment)
plot_subset <- dplyr::bind_rows(preds_labeled, obs_labeled) %>%
  dplyr::filter(subject_id %in% c(low_gamma_id, high_gamma_id))

# 3. Plot with consistent faceting
ggplot(plot_subset, aes(x = delta_val, y = rt, color = type)) +
  # Use geom_smooth to see the 'hump' clearly
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", alpha = 0.2) +
  # Add points with very low alpha to see the distribution spread
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~gamma_label, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "RT Hump Comparison by Subject Gamma",
    subtitle = "Observed vs. Predicted RT distributions",
    x = "Value Difference (Right - Left)",
    y = "Response Time (ms)",
    color = "Data Source"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

preds <- predict_glam(fit, processed_data, n_sims = 5)
plot_rt_fit(processed_data, preds) +
  labs(title = "Recovered RT Distribution",
       subtitle = "Model reproduces the RT peak at Delta Value = 0")
